Home › Apps › 3D Plantroom Designer
Service

3D Plantroom Designer

From a brief and a few site photos to a fully-coordinated 3D plantroom in hours, not weeks.

An agentic 3D modeller that designs the plantroom, sizes the equipment, routes the services, and hands you a Revit model ready for issue.

3 optionsdelivered per brief — pick or refine· Hoursnot weeks for a coordinated 3D layout· House librarypreferred manufacturers applied· Coordinatedagainst structure + arch envelope

Brief a plantroom → See related apps

Why use 3D Plantroom Designer

Send the project brief, the available plantroom footprint, the structural envelope, and the load schedule (or the agent will derive loads from the brief). The designer produces three options for the plantroom layout — typically: maintenance-optimised, footprint-optimised, and energy-optimised — each with a 3D model, a fully-sized plant item schedule, a service-route audit, and a coordination report against the structural model.

Every plant item is selected from your house preferred-manufacturer library and sized to the loads. Pipework, ductwork, and electrical containment are routed with maintenance access preserved (NHS HTM-08 / CIBSE Guide M clearances by default; configurable per practice). The designer audits its own work and tells you which assumptions it made.

For a typical commercial plantroom this collapses what is normally 1–2 weeks of senior MEP design effort into 2–4 hours of agent runtime plus a 30-minute review. Across a year, a five-engineer practice running 6–8 plantroom designs typically reclaims 8–12 weeks of senior-engineer time.

3D Plantroom Designer will be the only plantroom design service that gives you three coordinated options, in 3D, on the day you brief it.

The shift

What working looks like today versus with 3D Plantroom Designer.

Before

Plantrooms are days of senior-engineer iteration: sketch, model, re-sketch, re-route, miss something, re-do. Most projects ship a generic plantroom because nobody had time to design the right one.

After 3D Plantroom Designer

Send the brief, the loads, and the available footprint. Adelphos designs three plantroom options, sizes every plant item, routes the services, checks the maintenance access, and hands you a Revit model with a coordinated layout.

What makes it different

Features

What 3D Plantroom Designer does. Each line is a shipped capability.

3D Plantroom Designer capabilities

Three-option output Maintenance-optimised, footprint-optimised, energy-optimised — every brief, every time.
›
Plant item sizing Pumps, AHUs, chillers, boilers, calorifiers — sized to the loads, no rule-of-thumb.
›
Service routing in 3D Pipework, ductwork, electrical containment routed with maintenance access preserved.
›
Structure-coordinated Audited against the structural model and the architectural envelope.
›
House preferred manufacturers Selects equipment from your office library, not a generic catalogue.
›
Maintenance clearances HTM-08 / CIBSE Guide M defaults, configurable per practice.
›
Auto-derived loads If you send only the brief, the designer derives loads first; you can supply your own.
›
Coordination report Lists every assumption made, every clash resolved, every clearance applied.
›
Revit-ready output Returns a Revit model coordinated to your project template.
›

Install & system

Where 3D Plantroom Designer runs, what you need, and how to get it.

Service Run by Adelphos — no install. Brief → 3D model in 2–4 hours of runtime.
Web dashboard Review / refine / approve the three options in the browser, then export.

Requirements

  • An Adelphos account (free tier supports up to 2 plantroom briefs per month).
  • A project brief, structural envelope, and either a load schedule or enough info to derive one.
  • Optional: connect your house Revit template + preferred-manufacturer library.

Sign-in

Microsoft 365 SSO recommended.

Brief a plantroom → Read the docs first

Who it's for

MEP design leadsPlantroom-heavy schemesPractices issuing Stage 3 + plantroom layouts

Related

Feature PDF to 3D Model Conversion Converts 2D PDF drawings (and DWGs) into a fully-modelled, parameterised Revit model — built on your office template, with your families, ready for issue. App Revit Copilot Talk to Revit. The Copilot understands your model, applies your standards, and runs the 163 commands and 191 tools that ship with the platform. App Adelphos Chat Same agent. Same project context. Browser, phone, or desktop. The Adelphos Chat is the agent untethered from any one tool.

Frequently asked questions

Quick answers AI engines (and humans) can pull from.

What is 3D Plantroom Designer?

An agentic 3D modeller that designs the plantroom, sizes the equipment, routes the services, and hands you a Revit model ready for issue.

What does 3D Plantroom Designer do?

Send the project brief, the available plantroom footprint, the structural envelope, and the load schedule (or the agent will derive loads from the brief). The designer produces three options for the plantroom layout — typically: maintenance-optimised, footprint-optimised, and energy-optimised — each with a 3D model, a fully-sized plant item schedule, a service-route audit, and a coordination report against the structural model.

How is 3D Plantroom Designer different from the manual workflow?

Without 3D Plantroom Designer: Plantrooms are days of senior-engineer iteration: sketch, model, re-sketch, re-route, miss something, re-do. Most projects ship a generic plantroom because nobody had time to design the right one. With 3D Plantroom Designer: Send the brief, the loads, and the available footprint. Adelphos designs three plantroom options, sizes every plant item, routes the services, checks the maintenance access, and hands you a Revit model with a coordinated layout.

How much time does 3D Plantroom Designer save?

Headline outcomes for 3D Plantroom Designer: 3 options (delivered per brief — pick or refine); Hours (not weeks for a coordinated 3D layout); House library (preferred manufacturers applied); Coordinated (against structure + arch envelope).

Who is 3D Plantroom Designer for?

MEP design leads; Plantroom-heavy schemes; Practices issuing Stage 3 + plantroom layouts.

Brief a plantroom.

Built into the Adelphos platform. Self-serve where possible, managed where useful.
